page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-90 is a compute-focused server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 90 dedicated vCPUs derived from 45 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 360.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU accelerators, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations place this instance in the top 10% tier for multi-core CPU performance, software compilation with clang, and Passmark CPU encryption and extended instruction tests. Single-core processing, memory read bandwidth, and database operations score in the top 25% tier. However, memory write bandwidth for large uncached blocks and single-threaded decompression tasks perform in the middle 50% tier. Large-model LLM inference speeds also fall into the middle tier, with text generation for 70B models reaching 4.54 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3d-standard-90 offers high compute density and strong cryptographic and vector processing capabilities via AVX-512 and SHA extensions, without the added expense of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. The primary tradeoff is the reliance on external storage and the lack of hardware acceleration for heavy graphics or deep learning training. This configuration is optimized for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-90 is a compute-focused server instance featuring 90 dedicated vCPUs and 360.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for software compilation and cryptographic operations. It operates with a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. While memory read bandwidth and database operations are strong, large-block memory write bandwidth and large-model LLM text generation remain in the average tier. This instance is optimized for compute-intensive, multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, database operations, and static web serving where local storage is not a prerequisite.
